content: Donna Kelce home renovation memes while we're literally bombing Iran is the most American thing that has ever happened
images: Split screen editorial illustration: left side shows a cozy kitchen renovation with subway tile backsplash and granite counters, right side shows fighter jets over a desert landscape. Black and white with high contrast. Photojournalism style.
A phone screen showing a social media timeline with home renovation photos interspersed with breaking news alerts. Shot from above on a dark surface. Dramatic lighting.
Close-up photo of a gas station price sign showing absurdly high numbers, shot at night with harsh fluorescent lighting. Grain film aesthetic, moody.
A single gas pump nozzle dripping the last drop, dramatic black background, studio-lit like a luxury product photo. Ironic.

content: March Madness bracket already busted and it's been 4 hours
images: A crumpled up March Madness bracket sheet on a bar table next to a half-empty beer. Shallow depth of field, warm bar lighting.
Someone holding a printed bracket with angry red X marks all over it, face not visible, just hands and paper. Documentary style.

content: NYT Connections has replaced therapy for me and I am getting worse
images: A phone screen showing the NYT Connections game with a failed attempt, placed on a therapist's couch. Soft clinical lighting.
Close-up of someone's stressed face lit only by phone screen glow in a dark room at 2am. Blue light on skin.

content: genuinely cannot tell if my coworker's email was written by them or ChatGPT
images: An office laptop screen showing a suspiciously well-formatted email, with a person squinting at it skeptically. Shot from behind the screen.
Two identical emails side by side on a split monitor — one labeled HUMAN one labeled AI — they look exactly the same. Clean office lighting.

content: Daylight savings stole an hour from me on Sunday and I have been off by exactly one hour emotionally ever since
images: A clock showing the wrong time on a nightstand, person blurred in background looking confused. Early morning golden light through curtains.
Two clocks on a wall showing different times, one analog one digital. Moody shadows, minimal composition.

content: If you haven't tried the Costco chicken bake you're living a lesser life
images: A golden brown Costco chicken bake on a food court tray, shot like a Michelin star dish with dramatic plating angles. Dark background, single spotlight.
The Costco food court counter with the iconic menu board, shot from a customer's POV. Warm fluorescent glow, institutional beauty.

content: started using 'per my last email' genuinely instead of passive aggressively
images: Close-up of an email draft on screen with 'Per my last email' highlighted. Office keyboard visible. Cold blue monitor light.

content: the Donna Kelce renovation discourse outlasting actual geopolitical events on my timeline is why aliens won't visit
images: Night sky with stars, a single UFO turning around and heading away from Earth. Cinematic, wide shot, slightly humorous.
A phone showing a trending topics list where 'Donna Kelce Kitchen' is above 'Iran War Update'. Screen capture aesthetic.

content: just paid $7 for a single bell pepper at Whole Foods. seven dollars. for a pepper.
images: A single red bell pepper on a pedestal in a museum display case with a price tag showing $7.00. Gallery lighting, white walls, absurdist still life.
A receipt from Whole Foods with one line item circled: ORGANIC BELL PEPPER $6.99. Crumpled on a car dashboard.
